subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Introduction situates the film and clearly defines the focus on black-and-white cinematography and character construction.
Research question is precisely stated and consistently connected to every analytical section.
Overall methodology is coherent and well structured, with a logical outline and relevant primary and secondary sources.
weaknesses: Occasional digressions into broader Mexican cinema context dilute the laser-sharp focus.
Methodological reflection on scene selection is limited; criteria for choosing stills are not fully explained.
Conclusion does not explicitly restate the research question, missing an opportunity to close the investigative loop.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and research question focus) meet the 5â6 band while methodology is solid but uneven, fitting the 3â4 band. On balance the work demonstrates accurate and effective focus with only minor lapses, meriting 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates good knowledge of the filmâs context and relevant theorists (e.g., Bordwell, Aumont).
Correct and consistent use of technical film terminology throughout the analysis.
weaknesses: Integration of sources is often descriptive rather than critically synthesized, limiting academic rigor.
Use of non-peer-reviewed websites weakens the scholarly foundation; more journal articles or books are needed.
Inconsistent citation details (e.g., varying publication years for the same source) detract from scholarly precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands fall solidly in the 3â4 band: the essay shows good understanding and mostly accurate terminology but lacks consistently effective application of high-quality sources. A score of 4 out of 6 reflects competent but not exemplary knowledge and understanding.

strengths: Research is generally relevant, with primary scene analyses and secondary sources linked back to the research question.
Analysis makes clear connections between Aumontâs theory and specific film moments, showing thematic synthesis.
Insightful discussion of symbolism (e.g., the tiger metaphor) demonstrates strong engagement with the material.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation remains somewhat superficial, with limited interrogation of counter-arguments or alternative readings.
Many analytical claims are asserted rather than rigorously evidenced; deeper theoretical underpinning is needed.
Reliance on some non-scholarly sources suggests uneven critical selection of research materials.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands align with the 4â6 band and one with the 7â9 band; overall the essay achieves a consistent level of good critical thinking, warranting placement in the 7â9 band. An 8 out of 12 reflects solid research relevance and analysis depth tempered by only partially probing evaluation.

strengths: The structure fully meets IB requirements with all components (title page, contents, body, references) presented clearly.
Headings and subheadings are used consistently to guide the reader through the argument.
Pages are numbered and the layout is generally professional.
weaknesses: Section numbering contains minor inconsistencies that may confuse readers.
Occasional typographic errors and inconsistent indentation distract from overall polish.
Some figure captions precede their discussion, slightly impairing the flow of visual analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both presentation strands (structure and formatting) fall within the top band (3â4). Despite a few minor lapses in styling and numbering, the overall layout is clearly appropriate and complete, justifying 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Evidence of planning**: The student outlines a logical structure (introducciÃ³n, marco teÃ³rico, cuerpo de anÃ¡lisis, conclusiÃ³n) and compiles a bibliography of primary and secondary sources, showing an organised approach.

**Range and relevance**: While the essay cites a handful of academic texts (Bordwell, Aumont) and some online articles, the source base leans heavily on general websites and lacks depth in peer-reviewed scholarship. Film scenes are described, but there is limited methodological reflection on how they were selected or analysed.

Given this mostly complete yet uneven methodologyâwith some informed choices but clear gapsâthe work aligns with the middle 3â4 band; the higher criteria for clear evidence of *effective* selection are only partly met, justifying a score of 4.

**Formatting strengths**: The student provides a correctly formatted title page, consistent font, line spacing and numbered pages. Visual material is captioned and referenced, and the bibliography follows a recognisable citation style.

**Areas for improvement**: A few minor lapsesâsuch as inconsistent indentation, occasional over-sized images, and small typographic errorsâdetract slightly from overall polish, suggesting application is solid but not flawless.

These traits meet the descriptors for correct application of IB layout requirements, though not at a consistently exemplary level; hence a score of 3 within the 3â4 band.

**Breadth and relevance**: The student selects a reasonable spread of primary evidence (specific scenes, still images) and secondary sources that generally illuminate the research question. Most references tie back to black-and-white aesthetics, character theory, or Mexican cinema.

**Depth and reliability**: While some sources are scholarly, others are popular websites with variable authority. Citations are sometimes generic, suggesting partial but not comprehensive critical selection.

These qualities correspond to the 4â6 band, with the work leaning toward its upper half because the majority of research remains relevant and purposeful; hence a score of 5.

**Strengths**: The introduction clearly situates the film, the cinematic techniques under investigation and the scope of the study. The student immediately establishes that the essay will explore the interaction between black-and-white cinematography and character construction in *GÃ¼eros*, thereby signalling a precise focus and purpose.

**Weaknesses**: Although the topic is unmistakable, the phrasing occasionally drifts into general commentary on Mexican cinema, slightly diluting the laser-sharp sense of purpose expected at the very top of the band.

Overall, the topic is communicated **accurately and effectively**, with only minor lapses in concision, matching the expectations of the 5â6 band.

**Argument development**: The essay follows a logical progression from context to theory, analysis and conclusion, creating a coherent overarching argument about immersion in *GÃ¼eros*.

**Evaluation**: Critical reflection is presentâfor instance, acknowledging limitations of black-and-white as both homage and critiqueâbut evaluation of sources, counter-arguments or alternative readings remains surface-level. The conclusion synthesises rather than critically interrogates findings.

This places the discussion within the 4â6 band but at its lower end, equating to a score of 4 for partially critical yet not consistently probing evaluation.

**Analytical engagement**: The essay moves beyond plot summary to examine how cinematography and characterisation affect viewer immersion, and it offers concrete scene analyses (e.g., cassette-sharing moment, tiger symbolism).

**Support and coherence**: Nevertheless, many assertions are only loosely evidenced; quotations from theorists are paraphrased rather than critically interrogated, and the causal links between technique and audience response are asserted more than demonstrated.

The analysis is therefore **adequate** and occasionally insightful but lacks the sustained depth and weight of evidence found in the higher bands, justifying a mid-band score of 4.

**Structural compliance**: The essay contains all required EE componentsâtitle page with word count, abstract-style introduction, table of contents, main body, conclusion, and referencesâpresented in a clear, logical order.

**Internal organisation**: Headings and numbering guide the reader effectively, and paragraphing is generally coherent. Minor inconsistencies (duplicate section numbers, occasional mis-alignment) do not impede comprehension.

Given that the structure is **clearly appropriate and complete**, the work satisfies the top band (3-4) for this presentation strand; a score of 4 is awarded.

**Demonstrated knowledge**: The student summarises the filmâs plot, contextualises it within Mexican cinema, and references recognised theorists (e.g., Bordwell, Aumont). These elements show a solid, if not exhaustive, grasp of the topic and relevant scholarship.

**Application of sources**: Source material is used to support claimsâsuch as linking black-and-white imagery to Mexican Golden Age cinemaâbut the integration is often descriptive rather than critically synthesised. Some references (e.g., blog posts) limit academic rigour.

Consequently, the strand meets the descriptors of good but not exemplary knowledge and partially effective application of sources, meriting a score of 4 in the 3â4 band.

**Terminology use**: Technical film termsâsuch as âplano secuencia,â âinmersiÃ³n,â and âfotografÃ­a en blanco y negroââare employed correctly and woven naturally into the analysis.

**Conceptual accuracy**: Core literary-cinematic concepts like character construction, symbolism, and diegetic versus non-diegetic sound are addressed with reasonable accuracy. Occasional imprecision (e.g., conflating âformalismâ with Aristotelian notions) prevents full consistency.

Overall, terminology is **mostly accurate and appropriate**, aligning with the 3â4 band; a score of 4 reflects competent but not consistently precise usage.

**Clarity and focus**: The research question is expressly stated in bold on the title page and is tightly framed around two cinematic devices (photography and character construction) and two effects (narrative and immersion). This specificity keeps the investigation well-bounded.

**Connection to discussion**: Each analytical section (characters, visual elements, music, symbolism) is explicitly linked back to the ways photography and characterisation shape audience engagement, demonstrating an effective line of inquiry.

Minor digressions into historical context do not derail the main thread, so the strand comfortably fits the 5â6 band, warranting a score of 5.

annotations.comment: The language analysis is descriptive but lacks theoretical underpinning; integrating a linguistic or socio-semiotic theory would strengthen the arguments.

The research question is clearly and precisely stated, tightly framing the investigation around black-and-white cinematography and character construction. This focus guides the analysis effectively throughout the essay.
The table of contents is clearly organized with consistent formatting and page references, enhancing navigability of the essay.
Section numbering is inconsistent: the main analysis is labeled â3â after a prior â4â in the theoretical framework. Renumber sections sequentially to avoid reader confusion.
The student provides a word count at the start of the document, meeting the IB formatting requirement for transparency in report length.
Although the research question functions as a title, consider adding a concise descriptive title separate from the question to adhere more closely to IB title conventions.
Citation style for Bordwell (1995) is incomplete; include full publication details in the bibliography and ensure consistency with IB referencing guidelines.
The discussion of Sombraâs emotional journey is insightful but relies on broad statements. Anchoring claims with concrete scene references will deepen analytical rigor.
The selection of this still for analysis is justified by its emotional resonance, but the methodology for scene selection is not explained. Briefly outline criteria for choosing these images.
Analysis of silence effectively highlights emotional isolation, but referencing a sound design framework would enhance theoretical depth.

The introduction provides useful context on Mexican cinema, but it drifts into general commentary before linking directly back to the research focus, slightly diluting the laser-sharp sense of purpose.
This sentence ends abruptly and appears incomplete. Ensure that every paragraph conveys a complete idea to maintain clarity and coherence.
The discussion of formalist and psychological character theories lacks a citation for the Aristotelian framework. Providing a source strengthens academic rigor and contextualises the theory.

The claim that Ruizpalacios creates a hybrid approach would benefit from examples of specific scenes that demonstrate this theoretical blend, deepening the analytical connection between theory and film.
The year for Aumontâs work is cited variably as (1995) and (2002). Verify the correct publication date and apply it consistently throughout the text and references.
TomÃ¡sâs categorisation under Aristotelian character theory requires citation and evidence; consider quoting a film scholar who defines âimpulse charactersâ to substantiate this claim.
The heading â2.ConstrucciÃ³n de los personajesâ is missing a space after the numeral. Consistent typography of headings enhances professionalism.
The discussion of music presence and absence would benefit from citation of film sound theory (e.g., Chionâs work) to underpin the argument academically.
The symbol of Epigenio Cruz is identified but the evaluation remains surface-level. A deeper critique of memory and nostalgia themes would strengthen the critical evaluation.
The analysis of the tiger symbol is descriptive. To enhance depth, integrate a scholarly source on film symbolism to support your interpretation.
The subheading â2.2 Fedeâ uses inconsistent spacing and styling compared to other subheadings. Standardise heading formats across the document.
The bibliography relies heavily on non-peer-reviewed websites, which weakens scholarly rigor. Incorporate more academic journal articles or books for a stronger source base.
Additional reflections on source selection would demonstrate greater critical awareness of research quality and relevance, particularly distinguishing scholarly sources from popular media.
The analysis connecting Aumontâs theory to the headphone cassette scene is well-supported, illustrating how black-and-white cinematography intensifies emotional focus.
The subheading â5.2 El Tigreâ lacks consistent bold or underline styling. Ensure uniform formatting of all section headers.
Figure 3âs caption appears before its discussion in the text. Reordering elements so each figure is introduced before its caption will improve flow.
The discussion of the tigerâs metaphorical link to youthful potential and systemic constraints is insightful and demonstrates strong thematic synthesis.
The consistent use of headings and subheadings throughout the analysis provides a clear structural roadmap and enhances readability.
The conclusion summarises findings well but does not explicitly restate the research question. Reinforce the answer to the RQ to close the investigative loop.
Citation entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., missing publication information, year placement). Apply a single citation style uniformly across all references.
Correct application of technical terminology such as âplano secuenciaâ shows solid command of subject-specific vocabulary.
